title: layia glandulosa var. lutea
intro: Layia glandulosa var. lutea, also known as Golden Prairie Cup or Bush Sunflower, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Layia glandulosa var. lutea is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be made into a salve or infused oil for topical use.
